Methodology / Approach (Modules, Methods: • Core Technology: Agent-Based Modeling and Simulation (ABMS) paired with global optimization metaheuristics.• Operational Modules: Multi-agent behavioral engine representing doctors, triage nurses, and admission personnel as autonomous decision units; Exhaustive Search Optimization module.• Execution Schemes: Monte Carlo distribution replication paired with a Pipelined resource execution scheme.• Tech Stack: NetLogo agent simulation environment, Java-based operational interfaces.
Dataset Used: Real-world emergency department operational logs provided by the Hospital of Sabadell (Corporació Sanitària Parc Taulí) in Spain.
Key Findings (At least 3 main findings): 1. Staff Ratio Dominance: Simulation optimization proves that adding a single additional triage nurse reduces global patient length of stay (LOS) significantly more than adding multiple admission staff.2. Monte Carlo Consistency: Running parallel Monte Carlo iterations validates that the Pipelined agent layout achieves stable, predictable waiting times under variable patient arrival surges.3. Optimal Configuration Identification: The system successfully maps out a multi-objective cost-to-time index, identifying the exact tipping point where adding staff ceases to lower patient wait times.
Limitations: The NetLogo model relies on deterministic average treatment times per triage category, which can mask the true stochastic volatility of individual doctor workflows.
Future Scope: Extending the agent logic to model physical space limitations, such as active bed availability tracking and real-time laboratory test processing delays.

Paper Name: AI-Driven Triage: A Graph Neural Network Approach for Prehospital Emergency Triage Patients in IoMT-Based Telemedicine Systems
Methodology / Approach (Modules, Methods: • Core Technology: Spatial-Relational Graph Neural Network (GNN) processing multi-source streaming telemetry.• IoMT Architecture Stack: Real-time wearable biometric sensor integration arrays capturing continuous vital sign vectors.• Graph Topological Modules: Message-passing convolutional layers mapping patient states as nodes and dynamic relational histories as edges; Node classification output layer.• Tech Stack: Python, PyTorch Geometric (PyG), NetworkX graph layout library, and Scikit-Learn for baseline metric validation.
Dataset Used: Publicly available multi-source prehospital emergency triage benchmark datasets tracking volatile vital signs.
Key Findings (At least 3 main findings): 1. Topological Superiority: The GNN framework achieves higher triage prediction accuracy than non-relational models by capturing hidden spatial correlations between distinct physiological streams.2. IoMT Stream Synchronization: The message-passing architecture processes multi-sensor data streams with negligible transmission lag, making it highly viable for ambulance-to-hospital telemetry.3. Error Reduction: The model acts as an objective validator, successfully filtering out subjective bias and human recording errors common in high-stress manual triage.
Limitations: High computational dependency makes the GNN model vulnerable to severe network packet drops or sensory disconnects within the IoMT layer.
Future Scope: Deploying the GNN engine onto low-power edge computing hardware inside active emergency response vehicles for offline, real-time prehospital routing.

Paper Name: Autonomous Agentic AI for Clinical Workflow Orchestration: Self-Managing Healthcare Operations
Methodology / Approach (Modules, Methods: • Core Technology: Autonomous Agentic AI framework utilizing distributed multi-agent system (MAS) orchestration.• Agent Architecture Modules: Perception-Action loops, autonomous reasoning engines, and decentralized multi-agent contract negotiation protocols.• Orchestration Stack: Automated resource allocation modules, dynamic scheduling queues, and real-time cross-departmental communication buses.• Tech Stack: Python, LangChain/LangGraph agent orchestration layers, and custom message-queuing protocols (MQTT/AMQP).
Dataset Used: High-fidelity clinical workflow simulation data mapping patient journeys across triage, diagnostics, surgery, and discharge.
Key Findings (At least 3 main findings): 1. Workflow Efficiency: The self-managing agent system yields an estimated 15% to 20% reduction in total patient throughput times across highly congested clinical pathways.2. Dynamic Surge Adaptability: Decentralized agent negotiation allows the framework to dynamically reallocate beds and staff during sudden emergency surges without requiring human manual overrides.3. Silo Breakdown: The multi-agent contract protocol successfully automates coordination between isolated hospital departments, mitigating standard communication bottlenecks.
Limitations: The autonomous nature of the agent framework demands highly complex guardrails to ensure strict adherence to rigid medical safety compliance protocols.
Future Scope: Conducting extensive live clinical trials to evaluate the human-AI interaction dynamics between frontline medical staff and the autonomous orchestration agents.

Paper Name: Effective Approach to Reduce Patient Long Queues in Hospital Emergency Department
Methodology / Approach (Modules, Methods: • Core Technology: Quantitative Operations Research queuing model paired with technology-driven assessment optimization.• Queuing Modules: Multi-server queuing formulations (M/M/c and M/G/c) mapping arrival rates, service metrics, and queue lengths.• Analytical Stack: Statistical descriptive analysis module, service utilization capacity mapping, and bottleneck calculation blocks.• Tech Stack: SPSS statistical software package, Microsoft Excel optimization add-ins.
Dataset Used: Quantitative operational data collected via structured surveys and time-motion observations at St. Elizabeth Hospital in the Eastern Cape, South Africa.
Key Findings (At least 3 main findings): 1. Conventional Method Failure: Operational tracking proves that relying on traditional, unassisted FIFO queuing methodologies directly causes severe patient complications due to treatment delays.2. Utilization Imbalance: Bottleneck analytics reveal that the emergency department regularly operates beyond safe capacity limits due to an extreme shortage of automated triage sorting tools.3. Wait Time Optimization: Implementing technology-driven priority triage structures cuts average patient waiting room delays by a projected 30% without increasing staff headcount.
Limitations: The descriptive survey methodology relies heavily on self-reported retrospective feedback from respondents, which introduces potential recall bias.
Future Scope: Developing an interactive, real-time digital dashboard that links patient registration kiosks directly to live operational M/M/c queuing metrics.

Paper Name: Friends and enemies agents collaboration protocol to optimize multi-skills patient scheduling in emergency department
Methodology / Approach (Modules, Methods: • Core Technology: Multi-Agent System (MAS) running a custom "Friends and Enemies" collaboration optimization protocol.• Combinatorial Modules: Flexible Job Shop Scheduling formulation mapping a four-dimensional hypercube search space (Medical Staff, Patients, ED Structure, Time).• Metaheuristic Stack: Distributed Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and Genetic Algorithm (GA) blocks embedded directly within individual agent behaviors.• Tech Stack: Java Agent Development Framework (JADE), NetBeans IDE, and custom optimization libraries.
Dataset Used: Simulated combinatorial emergency department environment mapping highly variable multi-skilled staff attributes and fluctuating patient severities.
Key Findings (At least 3 main findings): 1. Combinatorial Resolution: The Friends and Enemies protocol successfully prevents agents from getting trapped in local minima, efficiently resolving an NP-hard scheduling problem.2. Severe Case Prioritization: The optimization objective effectively minimizes a global delay score, ensuring high-acuity patients receive immediate care while balancing low-acuity workflows.3. Metaheuristic Synergy: Combining cooperative (friends) and competitive (enemies) agent exploration outpaces traditional centralized metaheuristics in convergence speed by 15%.
Limitations: The hypercube solution space grows exponentially with hospital size, which can degrade real-time scheduling performance in mega-medical centers.
Future Scope: Integrating deep reinforcement learning loops into the individual agent structures to allow the scheduling system to learn from historical operational patterns.

Paper Name: Predictive and Personalized Healthcare Scheduling by Integrating No-Show Prediction with Artificial Intelligence
Methodology / Approach (Modules, Methods: • Core Technology: Integrated predictive machine learning pipeline paired with unsupervised behavioral clustering models.• Machine Learning Modules: XGBoost gradient boosting classifier, Deep Neural Network (DNN) sequential layers.• Clustering Architecture: K-Means and Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise (DBSCAN) modules for patient segmentation.• Feature Engineering Stack: Multi-source data ingestion pipeline processing demographic features, appointment records, real-time weather feeds, and social media engagement logs.• Tech Stack: Python, Scikit-Learn, XGBoost library, PyTorch, and Pandas.
Dataset Used: Large-scale multi-source clinical dataset tracking patient attendance records alongside environmental and platform engagement metrics.
Key Findings (At least 3 main findings): 1. Accurate Risk Forecasting: The hybrid XGBoost-DNN predictive engine achieves high sensitivity in identifying specific patient no-show risks long before the scheduled appointment time.2. Behavioral Segmentation: DBSCAN successfully isolates distinct patient clusters sharing identical attendance friction points, allowing for highly targeted communications.3. Resource Maximation: Implementing personalized, model-driven reminders yields a measurable increase in clinic resource utilization and minimizes wasted appointment slots.
Limitations: Incorporating external social media and engagement data requires strict data governance to protect patient privacy and maintain regulatory compliance.
Future Scope: Developing an automated, real-time dynamic appointment rescheduling engine that updates scheduling slots based on live no-show probability shifts.

Paper Name: Revolutionizing Healthcare Management: Architecture of a Web-Based Medical Triage Service
Methodology / Approach (Modules, Methods: • Core Technology: Cloud-native, scalable Web-based Medical Triage Service running a decentralized data architecture.• Storage & Analytics Stack: Data Lakehouse paradigm utilizing Delta Lake table storage over cloud object stores for high-performance, ACID-compliant data management.• Database Modules: MongoDB for flexible JSON-based patient record management; Apache Spark streaming engine for multilevel analytics.• Tech Stack: Node.js backend, React.js frontend, MongoDB Atlas, Delta Lake, Apache Spark, and RESTful API integration layers.
Dataset Used: Scalable architectural validation datasets simulating high-volume, concurrent user access requests under epidemic-level pandemic stress parameters.
Key Findings (At least 3 main findings): 1. Lakehouse Efficiency: Transitioning to a Data Lakehouse architecture successfully unifies real-time streaming triage telemetry with historical static records under a single governance model.2. ACID Transaction Security: Delta Lake integration guarantees absolute data consistency and zero transactional corruption during heavy concurrent database write surges.3. Operational Strain Relief: Scalability benchmarks prove the web-based system safely redistributes urgent care traffic, alleviating critical physical emergency room crowding.
Limitations: Web-based remote triage solutions require a reliable, high-speed internet connection from the end-user, limiting deployment in remote or underserved areas.
Future Scope: Integrating edge-based computer vision modules directly into the web interface to securely measure patient vital signs via the user's webcam.

Paper Name: Toward Improving Dynamic Resource Scheduling in the Context of Digital Twin of Emergency Department
Methodology / Approach (Modules, Methods: • Core Technology: Digital Twin (DT) decision-layer orchestration platform utilizing mathematical programming.• Scheduling Engine Module: Mixed Integer Linear Programming (MILP) model executing localized dynamic resource scheduling decisions.• Organizational Architectures: Comparative evaluation modules testing Decentralized Multi-Agent configurations against Centralized Global Optimization models.• Tech Stack: Java-based simulation engine, IBM ILOG CPLEX Optimization Studio for solving the underlying MILP matrices.
Dataset Used: Real-world operational flow tracking data from the pediatric Emergency Department of the University Hospital Centre in Saint-Étienne, France.
Key Findings (At least 3 main findings): 1. Decentralized Practicality: The decentralized MILP multi-agent system delivers scheduling performance that closely matches global centralized optimization while drastically reducing computing time.2. FIFO Outperformance: Both the centralized and decentralized MILP digital twin models thoroughly outclass the traditional FIFO sequencing currently used in French pediatric care.3. Real-Time Responsiveness: The digital twin architecture successfully ingests close-to-real-time data streams, transforming static scheduling assumptions into dynamic workflows.
Limitations: The mathematical optimization model requires highly accurate, continuous real-time input data; lag within the digital twin tracking layer degrades scheduling precision.
Future Scope: Expanding the optimization parameters to dynamically account for highly volatile staff fatigue levels and sudden equipment breakdowns.

Methodology / Approach (Modules, Methods: • Core Technology: Automated Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) interactive AI triage assistant framework.• Authentication Module: Facial recognition biometric matching network combined with standard secure user ID/password authentication protocols.• Multimodal Interaction Stack: Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) voice processing engine, secure interactive text chat, and video conversation interfaces.• Telemetry & Analytics Modules: Remote photoplethysmography (rPPG) facial video vital sign extractor paired with a dynamic text-summarization pipeline (Chatsum).• Tech Stack: Python, CoreML/OpenCV for face tracking, custom NLP text summarization blocks, and a secure cloud-hosted SaaS infrastructure.
Dataset Used: Experimental and simulation datasets tracking interactive multi-modal dialogue logs, clinical summaries, and video-based physiological extractions.
Key Findings (At least 3 main findings): 1. Data Collection Automation: Triage-Bot successfully automates initial patient intake, gathering vital signs and medical histories completely hands-free before a nurse arrives.2. Summary Synthesis Efficiency: The embedded Chatsum tool accurately distills highly rambling verbal patient complaints into highly concise, structured medical summaries for clinicians.3. Contactless Vitals Reliability: The integration of rPPG computer vision modules enables secure, remote extraction of baseline pulse metrics directly from the patient's face video input.
Limitations: Subtle facial movements, low-resolution camera inputs, and changes in ambient room lighting can interfere with the accuracy of the rPPG vital signs extractor.
